design_philosophy.core_loop: Fly freely in 2D space, rotate aim independently from movement, defeat enemies for currency, purchase weapon upgrades from shops between stages, face boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Decoupled movement and aiming creates strategic positioning puzzle; resource management (Zenny for upgrades) gates power progression; megacrush risk-reward mechanic trades vitality for screen clear
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Supersoldier with perfect aim control navigating alien wasteland with orbital satellite companion; god-like precision in combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rotatable aim independent from movement direction is signature mechanic—a deliberate design choice to make shmup shooting less about twitch reflexes and more about spatial reasoning and planned positioning. Game explicitly designed to be easier than prior Capcom shooters to reduce dodging difficulty. Shop system for weapon customization breaks from arcade shooter conventions of fixed loadouts.

difficulty.lives_system: Vitality gauge representing player health; character loses when gauge depletes
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system present; player given chance to continue after defeat
difficulty.checkpointing: Nine stages with boss encounters at end of each; progression is linear stage-by-stage
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through shop system—player chooses which weapon upgrades and items (health kits, armor) to purchase between stages using earned currency (Zenny), allowing customization of loadout difficulty. Megacrush mechanic provides trade-off: spending vitality for screen clear is optional risk-reward. Difficulty hints available for purchase from shops.

progression.upgrade_system: Currency-based (Zenny coins dropped by defeated enemies); players spend at shops to purchase: new satellite weapons, health restoration, armor (damage reduction), and boss strategy hints
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression is linear; nine stages unlock sequentially. Weapon variety unlocks through shop purchases rather than progression gates
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon loadout combinations via shop purchases create different playstyle variations
Continue system allows experimentation with different upgrade strategies
Two-player simultaneous mode with asymmetric weapons (P1: long-range rifle vs P2: short-range wide shot) creates distinct experiences

world_building_techniques: Plot framing: 29th century Earth destroyed by evil god Bios, transformed into Dust World wasteland
Character premise: two nameless supersoldiers created to defeat Bios and eight evil gods
Minimal narrative delivery; story serves as thematic backdrop rather than active gameplay driver

boss_design.philosophy: Nine stage bosses serve as progression milestones; boss strategy hints purchasable from shops suggests pattern-recognition difficulty rather than pure execution. Boss designs leverage the decoupled aiming mechanic—defeating them requires mastering directional aim while maintaining optimal positioning.
notable_bosses: Eight evil god servitors (one per stage boss, implied)

faction_indicators: Enemy forces of Bios (implied alien/demonic faction)
danger_signals: Dust World wasteland setting signals environmental catastrophe
Enemy projectiles and patterns indicate aggressive opposition
Boss encounters frame escalating threat level
environmental_storytelling: Desolate Dust World aesthetic conveys post-apocalyptic setting
Stage-by-stage progression through destroyed Earth implies exploration of ruined civilization
Shop locations suggest pockets of surviving infrastructure/commerce
